description:
**Problem**
Daemon code in \`beads-rs/src/daemon/\` calls \`crate::paths::store_dir()\`, \`crate::config::*\`, etc. directly. When we move the daemon implementation into the \`beads-daemon\` crate, it cannot depend on \`beads-rs\` (forbidden edge). Need to break the coupling first with injection types.

**Design**
Add typed configuration/layout structs to \`beads-daemon\` that the daemon runtime accepts as parameters instead of reaching into beads-rs internals.

\`\`\`rust
// crates/beads-daemon/src/layout.rs
pub struct DaemonLayout {
    pub data_dir: PathBuf,
    pub socket_path: PathBuf,
    pub stores_dir: PathBuf,
}
impl DaemonLayout {
    pub fn store_dir(&self, store_id: &StoreId) -> PathBuf;
    pub fn wal_dir(&self, store_id: &StoreId) -> PathBuf;
    pub fn log_dir(&self) -> PathBuf;
}

// crates/beads-daemon/src/config.rs
pub struct DaemonRuntimeConfig {
    pub limits: beads_core::Limits,
    pub namespace_defaults: BTreeMap<NamespaceId, NamespacePolicy>,
    pub checkpoint_groups: BTreeMap<String, CheckpointGroupConfig>,
    pub replication: ReplicationConfig,
    pub git_sync_policy: GitSyncPolicy,
    pub checkpoint_policy: CheckpointPolicy,
}
\`\`\`

Thread these through daemon constructors, replacing \`crate::paths::*\` calls. Update \`beads-rs::run_daemon_command()\` to construct from config/paths and pass in.

Key hotspots:
- \`StoreRuntime::open(...)\` — computes store_dir internally
- WAL segment paths — derived from store_dir
- Socket path resolution — for IPC listener
- Log directory — for daemon log files

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`DaemonLayout\` struct in \`beads-daemon\` with store_dir/wal_dir/log_dir methods
- [ ] \`DaemonRuntimeConfig\` struct in \`beads-daemon\` capturing all runtime config
- [ ] \`beads-rs::run_daemon_command()\` constructs both from config/paths and passes to daemon
- [ ] No daemon code directly calls \`crate::paths::*\` — all through DaemonLayout
- [ ] \`cargo check\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test\` passes
- [ ] IPC behavior unchanged (integration tests green)

**Files:** \`crates/beads-daemon/src/lib.rs\`, \`crates/beads-daemon/src/layout.rs\` (new), \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core/mod.rs\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/run.rs\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/store/runtime.rs\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs\`

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
**Scope note for implementing agent:**

Actual callsite counts (from `rg` on current tree):
- `crate::paths` — 24 callsites across 14 files (heaviest: store/runtime.rs, core/mod.rs, admin/)
- `crate::config` — 23 callsites across 8 files (heaviest: store/runtime.rs has 11 alone, core/mod.rs has 4)

The config types you'll need (`CheckpointGroupConfig`, `ReplicationConfig`, `GitSyncPolicy`, `CheckpointPolicy`) partially exist in `crates/beads-rs/src/config/schema.rs` and `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core/mod.rs`. You'll need to either:
1. Define clean equivalents in beads-daemon (preferred — don't import beads-rs config types)
2. Or move the relevant config schema types down to beads-daemon

Also note `store/runtime.rs` has the heaviest coupling — 11 config imports + 2 paths imports. Start there.

title: Add DaemonLayout + DaemonRuntimeConfig injection types to beads-daemon

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Segment writers are reused across multiple events (not one segment per mutation).
- [ ] A single EventWal append API owns the write + fsync + index sequencing invariant.
- [ ] Executor and replication ingest no longer manually open SegmentWriter directly.
description:
**Problem**
Every mutation opens a fresh `SegmentWriter` and creates a new WAL segment, so we effectively write one segment per event. There is no `EventWal` type that owns per‑namespace writers and enforces the append/index/fsync ordering invariant; instead, the executor and core manually sequence these steps.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `apply_mutation_request_inner` creates a new `SegmentWriter::open` for every mutation (`src/daemon/executor.rs`).
- `ingest_remote_batch` also opens a new `SegmentWriter` per batch (`src/daemon/core.rs`).
- `wal/mod.rs` exposes low‑level pieces but no high‑level append API; the invariants live in call sites.

**Why this hurts velocity**
We keep duplicating WAL/index sequencing logic, and segment rotation logic never gets a chance to do its job. Any change to WAL invariants requires editing multiple call sites and is easy to get subtly wrong.
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an `EventWal` (or `WalAppender`) struct in `src/daemon/wal` that owns per‑namespace active `SegmentWriter`s.
- Provide append APIs that encapsulate: (1) allocate seq, (2) write record, (3) fsync, (4) index updates, (5) watermark updates.
- Keep a per‑namespace writer in `StoreRuntime` (or in EventWal) so segments are reused and rotation is meaningful.
- Update mutation and replication ingest to go through this API.

**Design Notes**
This aligns with the plan’s “EventWal::append” responsibility and moves invariants into one type instead of scattered call sites.
title: Introduce EventWal append API + reuse segment writers

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Roster update + reload path exercised in e2e.
- [ ] Store_epoch bump path exercised; peers reconnect without data loss.
- [ ] Replication converges post-change.
- [ ] Tests write only under ./tmp.
description:
**Problem**
We do not test dynamic replica roster changes or store_epoch bumps. These are high-risk paths for long-running deployments and upgrades.
design:
**Design**
- Add integration helpers to update roster (and/or replication config) at runtime and trigger a reload via admin command.
- If no admin API exists to bump store_epoch, add one (or document the intended mechanism), then simulate an epoch bump and ensure peers reconnect.
- Add slow e2e that changes roster/epoch mid-flight and asserts continued convergence.
title: Roster/epoch change e2e for replication
